[[Willie Raston]] (nephew) }} }} '''David Walter Lomax''' (born 21 September 1970)[http://nzleague.co.nz/kiwis/player_profile.php?letter=L&id=652 LOMAX, DAVID WALTER 1993 - KIWI #652] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120402171255/http://nzleague.co.nz/kiwis/player_profile.php?letter=L&id=652 |date=2 April 2012 }} ''nzleague.co.nz'' is a New Zealand [[rugby league]] coach and former player who represented [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]]. He is the brother of another international, [[John Lomax (rugby league)|John Lomax]]. ==Early years== Lomax grew up playing rugby league for the [[Wainuiomata Lions]] in the [[Wellington Rugby League]] competition and also represented Wellington at a provincial level. During the 1992 season he lined up alongside three of his brothers; Tony, [[John Lomax (rugby league)|John]] and Arnold, for Wellington against [[Bay of Plenty Rugby League|Bay of Plenty]].Coffey and Wood ''The Kiwis: 100 Years of International Rugby League'' {{ISBN|1-86971-090-8}} All four brothers also played for the Lions that year in their 25-18 national club grand final win over the [[Northcote Tigers]]. He represented the [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team|New Zealand Māori]] side in 1993.{{cite book |first1=John|last1=Coffey|first2=Bernie|last2=Wood |title = 100 years: Māori rugby league, 1908-2008 |publisher =Huia Publishers |year = 2008 |page = 280 |url = https://books.google.com/books?id=nklWo8vw-iIC |isbn =978-1-86969-331-2}} ==Playing career== He joined the [[Canberra Raiders]] about the same time as his brother [[John Lomax (rugby league)|John]]. However he could not break into the first grade team at Canberra and soon found himself moving around, spending seasons at the [[WA Reds|Western Reds]] and [[Paris Saint-Germain Rugby League|Paris Saint-Germain]] before settling at the [[Newcastle Knights]] where he played 29 matches. He then moved again, joining the merged [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]]-[[Sheffield Eagles|Sheffield]] side before retiring. ==Representative career== During the 1993 season, Lomax made the [[New Zealand national rugby league team]], playing in two test matches. ==Coaching career== He later coached the [[Central Falcons]] in the [[Bartercard Cup]], making sporadic appearances for the side off the bench.[http://www.accessmylibrary.com/coms2/summary_0286-21653490_ITM David Lomax does the double] ''The Dominion Post'', 14 June 2004 In 2006 he was the coach of the [[New Zealand Residents]] side that competed in the Trans Tasman Quadrangular Series.[http://www.qrl.com.au/display.php?pg_id=1916 Rangers Bowed in Opening Battle] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090925122134/http://www.qrl.com.au/display.php?pg_id=1916 |date=2009-09-25}} ''QRL.com'', Accessed 2 August 2009 In 2007 he was appointed coach of the [[Junior Kiwis]].[http://www.rleague.com/db/article.php?id=29319 Stanley set to line up for Junior Kiwis] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100212065525/http://rleague.com/db/article.php?id=29319 |date=12 February 2010 }} ''NZRL (Press Release)'', 11 October 2007 He applied for the [[New Zealand national rugby league team]] coaching job in mid-2007 and was shortlisted however the job ultimately went to [[Gary Kemble]].{{cite web|url=http://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/news/article.cfm?c_id=4&objectid=10454469|title=League: Leuluai a last-minute candidate|author=Brown, Michael|date=29 July 2007|work=[[The New Zealand Herald]]|access-date=27 September 2011}} Lomax then relocated to Wellington where he coached the Te Aroha Eels in 2008 and 2009 before taking over the Porirua Vikings. Lomax is coaching [[Wellington rugby league team|Wellington]] in the 2012 [[NZRL Men's National Competition|National Competition]]. ==References== {{reflist|2}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Lomax, David}} [[Category:1970 births]] [[Category:Living people]] [[Category:20th-century New Zealand sportsmen]] [[Category:Canberra Raiders players]] [[Category:Expatriate rugby league players in France]] [[Category:Huddersfield Giants players]] [[Category:Junior Kiwis coaches]] [[Category:Manawatu rugby league team coaches]] [[Category:Manawatu rugby league team players]] [[Category:New Zealand expatriate rugby league players in Australia]] [[Category:New Zealand expatriate rugby league players in England]] [[Category:New Zealand expatriate rugby league players in France]] [[Category:New Zealand Māori rugby league players]] [[Category:New Zealand Māori rugby league team players]] [[Category:New Zealand national rugby league team players]] [[Category:New Zealand rugby league coaches]] [[Category:New Zealand rugby league players]] [[Category:Newcastle Knights players]] [[Category:Paris Saint-Germain Rugby League players]] [[Category:Rugby league players from Wellington City]] [[Category:Rugby league props]] [[Category:Sportspeople from Levin, New Zealand]] [[Category:Wainuiomata Lions players]] [[Category:Wellington rugby league team coaches]] [[Category:Wellington rugby league team players]] [[Category:Western Reds players]]
